data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-57faa17c">
    <title>server: reconcile loop kills fresh server-managed workers ~5s after bead claim (spawn/kill churn every tick)</title>
    <description>
# PROBLEM
Observed live on eitri 2026-07-05 20:40-21:00 EDT (ddx v0.6.2-alpha104-4162-g772c3f55a): for every project with ready beads (ddx, snorri, pqueue), the supervisor spawns a fresh `ddx work --server-managed` worker on its 10s tick; the worker claims the top ready bead, prints 'readiness check: starting', then ~5s later receives SIGINT ('Cancel received, shutting down gracefully', exit status 130) and dies. The next tick spawns a replacement, which repeats the cycle. No bead ever executes; each cycle can invoke the codex-backed pre-claim intake hook, burning provider spend every 10 seconds per project. The cayce worker — whose queue was empty and which therefore never claimed — survived the whole window, so the kill correlates with the claim path, not worker age. Worker record example: /Users/erik/Projects/snorri/.ddx/workers/worker-20260706T004055-5645 (state=failed, last_error='exit status 130', log ends 'readiness check: starting' → 'Cancel received').

This is a distinct manifestation from ddx-c8506158 (duplicate long-lived workers on sindri after handle loss): here the workers hold live in-memory handles (spawned by the current server process) and are killed, not duplicated.

# ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS
Something in the per-tick reconcile path revokes the fresh worker's claim or stops it by ID. Grep entry points:
- cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:253-260 ReconcileAt: every tick, staleIDs (running-on-disk, no in-memory handle) go through stopStaleDiskEntry.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:1406-1459 stopStaleDiskEntry: unclaims the bead recorded on the stale record (`store.Unclaim(beadID)`) — with ~150 historical worker dirs per project, a stale record naming the SAME top-ready bead the live worker just claimed would release the live worker's claim out from under it; the worker's claim-guard then cancels the attempt (matches the observed SIGINT-equivalent graceful cancel). Verify whether stopStaleDiskEntry can fire repeatedly (writeRecord failure leaves state=running) or whether many distinct stale records reference the current top bead.
- cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor.go:286-344 snapshotWorkers: confirm hasWorkerHandle is true for subprocess-launched workers of the CURRENT server generation; if false they land in staleIDs and get stop-stale'd (claim released + record flipped) within one tick of spawning — which matches the observed ≤10s lifetime exactly.

# PROPOSED FIX
Determined by which hypothesis verifies. Candidates: make snapshotWorkers classify current-generation subprocess workers via OS-liveness (kill(pid,0)) instead of in-memory handle only; make stopStaleDiskEntry skip unclaim when the bead's current claim does not belong to the stale record's worker/attempt; one-shot stale sweep at startup instead of per-tick.

# NON-SCOPE
- ddx-c8506158 (adopt-or-terminate handle-less duplicates across restarts) — sibling, same root-cause area but different invariant.
- workerprobe TLS registration failure (ddx-a6e99ad3).
- Operator-attention relaunch suppression not being honored (separate bead).

Related: ddx-c8506158, ddx-a6e99ad3.

No deps.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. New test TestSupervisorReconcile_FreshClaimingWorkerSurvivesTicks in cli/internal/server/ proves: a subprocess-launched worker of the current manager generation that has claimed a bead survives >=3 consecutive ReconcileAt ticks (not stopped, claim intact).
2. New test TestStopStaleDiskEntry_DoesNotUnclaimForeignClaim proves: stopStaleDiskEntry on a stale record whose bead is currently claimed by a DIFFERENT live worker/attempt does not release that claim.
3. Live verify on eitri documented in closing note: with desired_count=1 restored for ddx/snorri/pqueue, workers survive >10 minutes (pgrep count stable across ticks) and at least one bead reaches a terminal outcome (success/no_changes/etc), no exit-130 churn in .ddx/workers records.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'Supervisor|StaleDisk' -count=1 green
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:server, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, phase:build, plan:server-managed-workers, supervisor:duplicate</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-9d1af129" base-rev="57e3dcfee3d1bdecfb1213e04fb852dd6156aa77" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260706T010755-dde002e0"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Committed as `beae546b2` with message `fix: preserve fresh bead claims during stale worker cleanup [ddx-57faa17c]`.

What changed:
- [workers.go](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-57faa17c-20260706T010755-dde002e0/cli/internal/server/workers.go#L1406) now skips `Unclaim` when the bead still has a fresh claim lease, so stale disk entries stop without ripping ownership away from a live worker.
- [workers_prune_test.go](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-57faa17c-20260706T010755-dde002e0/cli/internal/server/workers_prune_test.go#L233) now covers both stale-release and foreign-live-claim cases.
- [workers_supervisor_test.go](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-57faa17c-20260706T010755-dde002e0/cli/internal/server/workers_supervisor_test.go#L187) now proves reconcile leaves a fresh claiming worker alone across multiple ticks.
- [workers_resolve_route_test.go](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-57faa17c-20260706T010755-dde002e0/cli/internal/server/workers_resolve_route_test.go#L185) got a test-timeout increase so the server package hook is stable on this host.

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run 'Supervisor|StaleDisk' -count=1`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

I did not perform the live eitri verification from AC3 in this workspace.
